opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Parent: 'He has had a burning fever for six days that Panadol won't touch, his eyes are bloodshot, and his lips are bleeding.'
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Six days of high unremitting fever with red eyes, cracked bleeding lips, a rash, refusal to walk, and now sudden lethargy and a crying episode clutching his chest.

symptom_narrative.duration: 6 days of persistent high fever (>39 C) unresponsive to alternating paracetamol and ibuprofen
symptom_narrative.character: burning fever with bloodshot eyes, deeply red cracked bleeding lips, a rash on the trunk, and swollen sore hands and feet
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: parents are highly distressed by his continuous decline; he looks toxic and is inconsolable
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: whole body fever; red eyes; lips; rash on trunk; swollen hands and feet; today an episode of crying and clutching his chest
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: fever unrelenting for 6 days; lips cracked yesterday; today unusually lethargic and irritable with a sudden chest-clutching cry an hour ago
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: bloodshot eyes without discharge, cracked bleeding lips, rash, swollen sore hands/feet, refusing to walk or eat, and a sudden episode of chest clutching
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: none - previously well and fully vaccinated
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: lethargic, refusing to walk or eat, inconsolable

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with exhausted, highly distressed parents; fully vaccinated

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: exhausted, frightened parents watching their child decline over days despite treatment

communication_style.typical_message_style: distressed parents relaying the history; child inconsolable

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent-measured; fever persistently >39 C for 6 days
